metadata.working_title: Digital telomere measurement by long-read sequencing distinguishes healthy aging from disease

abstract_sketch.problem: Telomere length is a biomarker of organismal aging and cellular replicative potential, but existing measurement methods (TRF, flow-FISH, qPCR, STELA, TeSLA) are limited in resolution and accuracy.
abstract_sketch.gap: Prior assays either yield coarse mean telomere lengths, relative content estimates, or rely on PCR amplification that preferentially reports the shortest telomeres; none resolves the full distribution of individual telomere lengths with single-telomere precision.
abstract_sketch.approach: We deploy digital telomere measurement by nanopore long-read sequencing combined with a telomere-capture oligonucleotide library preparation and the Telometer bioinformatic pipeline to recover intact, chromosome-anchored telomere reads from either whole-genome or enriched sequencing libraries.
abstract_sketch.key_results: The method reaches a maximal precision of 30-40 bp, correlates with TRF (n=14) and flow-FISH (R2=0.84, n=7), detects ~40 bp/day attrition in TERT-null hESCs, measures ~1000 bp de novo elongation after 3 days of telomerase overexpression, recapitulates ~24 bp/year attrition in PBLs of 14 healthy donors, and supports a machine-learning classifier distinguishing healthy donors from TBD carriers (AUC=0.93), patients (AUC=0.98), and combined cases (AUC=0.91); tumor telomeres were shorter than matched benign epithelia in 75% of 20 colorectal carcinoma patients.
abstract_sketch.impact: Single-molecule telomere distributions reveal that the interquartile structure encodes the state of the telomere maintenance machinery, enable detection of asymptomatic TBD carriers undetectable by flow-FISH, and establish digital telomere measurement as a clinical biomarker platform for aging and disease.

sections.title: Introduction
content_bullets: Telomeres are essential protective structures at chromosome ends whose attrition with division limits replicative potential and contributes to aging.
Existing telomere length assays (TRF Southern blot, flow-FISH, qPCR, STELA, TeSLA) only provide coarse mean lengths, relative content, or biased views of the shortest fragments.
Long-read sequencing (PacBio HiFi, Oxford Nanopore) now offers the resolution to measure individual intact telomeres anchored to specific chromosomes.

Gap: no existing method recovers the full distribution of individual telomere lengths with the throughput needed for clinical cohorts or the precision needed to distinguish healthy aging from disease carriers.

Contribution: digital telomere measurement via nanopore sequencing combined with telomere-capture enrichment (~several thousand-fold) and the Telometer bioinformatic pipeline for chromosome-anchored telomere quantification.

We validate the method against TRF and flow-FISH, apply it to cultured hESC models of TBD genotypes, to PBLs from 14 healthy donors spanning ages 18-77, to 7 RTEL1-mutant individuals plus a CMML patient with a novel TINF2 frameshift, and to 20 matched colorectal carcinoma samples.
Roadmap: validation, aging, disease classification, cancer cohort, and clinical/biological implications.

sections.title: Related Work
content_bullets: Analog telomere measurement: Southern blot (TRF), fluorescence in situ hybridization (flow-FISH, Q-FISH), qPCR - strengths and limitations.
PCR-based short-telomere assays: STELA and TeSLA target the shortest fraction but are limited by polymerase processivity.
Prior long-read sequencing of telomeres using PacBio HiFi and Oxford Nanopore platforms, and reference-genome advances including the telomere-to-telomere (T2T) human genome.
Short-read sequencing approaches such as TelSeq estimate relative telomeric content in cohorts such as TCGA.
Telomere biology disorders (dyskeratosis congenita, RTEL1-related disease) and the clinical diagnostic pathway combining flow-FISH with targeted exon sequencing.

subsections.title: Digital telomere measurement by long-read sequencing measures intact telomeres at high resolution
content_bullets: Telometer pipeline defines each telomere from the terminal repeat to the final two consecutive repeats preceding the subtelomere anchor on the T2T reference.
Telomere capture oligo targeting the 3' overhang plus restriction digestion enriches telomeric reads several thousand-fold without distorting the length distribution (Fig. 1B-C).
Digital mean telomere lengths correlate with TRF (n=14, Fig. 1D-E) and flow-FISH (n=7, Fig. 1F-G); TRF systematically overestimates by 1000-3000 bp.
Bootstrapping shows the standard error of the mean decays exponentially with additional telomere measurements, plateauing at a maximal precision of 30-40 bp (Fig. 1H).
Digital measurement discriminates wild-type from pathologically short PARN knock-out (p < 2e-16, Wilcoxon rank-sum test) and TIN2 T284R mutant hESCs.

subsections.title: Digital telomere measurement captures attrition and de novo elongation in cultured cells
content_bullets: TERT knockout hESCs (CDKN2A-/- TERT-/-) passaged 66, 78, 98, 105 days post Cre-mediated inactivation show progressive shortening at ~40 bp per day (Fig. 2B-C).
TIN2 T284R heterozygous and homozygous hESCs exhibit progressively shorter distributions relative to wild-type, consistent with the dominant-negative shelterin phenotype.
Transient transfection of HEK293T with hTR or TSQ1 plus TERT increases telomerase activity (TRAP) and yields a ~1000 bp increase in mean telomere length in 3 days (Fig. 2E-H).

Chromosome-anchored analysis reveals that chromosomes with shorter first-quartile telomeres elongate most under telomerase overexpression (Fig. 2I), providing evidence of preferential action of telomerase on short telomeres.
Detection of variant telomere repeats (TSQ1, TTGCGG) via variant-specific capture confirms the method resolves full-length, de novo telomere additions.

subsections.title: High-resolution telomere measurement distinguishes human aging and disease
content_bullets: PBLs from 14 healthy donors (age 18-77) show that mean, median, Q1, and Q3 telomere lengths decline with age (~24 bp/year for mean/median), with Q3 decreasing more steeply than Q1 (Fig. 3A, D-E, H).
Cohort aggregation (young 18-20, n=5; middle 35-65, n=6; elder >70, n=3) confirms progressive loss of long telomeres and accumulation of shorter fractions.

RTEL1-mutant individuals (n=7) and a Stanford patient with a novel TINF2 p.Gln298fs frameshift, CMML, and pulmonary fibrosis show significantly shorter telomeres than age-matched healthy donors (Fig. 3B-C, F) and strong correlation with flow-FISH mean lengths (R2=0.84).
PCA of telomere distributions separates healthy (n=14) from TBD carrier samples (n=9) (Fig. 3G); the shortest two 1000-bp bins are enriched in TBD samples (Fig. 3H-I).

A binary classifier trained on telomere measurements and age achieves 95% accuracy (AUC=0.98) for healthy vs symptomatic patients, 91% (AUC=0.93) for healthy vs asymptomatic carriers, and 86% (AUC=0.91) for healthy vs combined cases (Fig. 3J).

subsections.title: Telomere length distributions in patient-matched colorectal carcinoma and benign epithelia
content_bullets: Whole-genome long-read data (30X average coverage) from 20 patients (predominantly 50-70 years) with matched benign colon and colorectal tumor tissue were reanalyzed.
Benign colonic epithelium shows age-related shortening with Q3 declining faster than Q1; tumor telomeres show no correlation with donor age.
Tumor telomeres are significantly shorter than matched benign epithelia in 75% of patients (Wilcoxon rank-sum test per patient).

sections.title: Discussion
content_bullets: Interpretation: digital telomere measurement surpasses TRF and flow-FISH by delivering the full distribution at 30-40 bp precision with chromosome-level anchoring, while requiring less input DNA than either.

Mechanism: the interquartile structure of the telomere length distribution encodes the state of the telomere maintenance apparatus - limiting telomerase (and not negative selection) appears to drive accumulation of the shortest fractions with age.

Clinical implication: the classifier distinguishes asymptomatic RTEL1 carriers from healthy donors with AUC=0.93, addressing a blind spot of flow-FISH in carrier diagnosis and supporting genetic-anticipation-aware triage.

Novel biology: preferential elongation by telomerase of chromosomes with shorter Q1 telomeres, and stable tumor distributions despite attrition in matched benign tissue (75% of 20 patients), point to a tunable telomeric rheostat.

Unexpected observation: measurable telomeres as short as tens of base pairs are present in every sample, including hESCs, suggesting shelterin-mediated tolerance of extremely short telomeres below a senescence threshold.
Limitations: cross-sectional cohort, relatively small TBD sample (n=9 variant carriers), reliance on capture efficiency that may vary by telomere state.

Future directions: longitudinal cohorts, tissue-resolved aging panels, integration with single-cell readouts, investigation of the shelterin/RNA-template/telomerase dosage contributions to the rheostat setpoint, and use of telomere distributions as predictive biomarkers in cancer.

sections.title: Methods
content_bullets: Sample sources: HEK293T cells; wild-type, PARN KO, TIN2 T284R het/hom, and TERT knockout hESCs; PBLs from 14 healthy donors aged 18-77; PBLs from 6 individuals with RTEL1 mutations (7 samples, ages 6-50); paired PBL (TB32-B) and bone marrow (TB32-M) DNA from a Stanford CMML/pulmonary-fibrosis patient with TINF2 p.Gln298fs; public 30X WGS from 20 matched colorectal carcinoma and benign epithelia.

Telomere capture: biotinylated oligonucleotides complementary to the 3' overhang (canonical 5'-TTAGGG-3' or variant 5'-TTGCGG-3') combined with restriction digestion; library loaded on Oxford Nanopore platform.

Bioinformatic pipeline (Telometer): align telomeric repeats to T2T human reference termini; define telomere extent from the terminal repeat to the last two consecutive repeats before the subtelomere anchor; compute per-read length and per-sample distributions.

Cell culture and genetics: TERT inactivation via Cre recombinase in CDKN2A-/- TERT-/- AAVSI:hTERTflox hESCs passaged for 66, 78, 98, 105 days; transient transfection of HEK293T with hTR, TSQ1, or GFP plus TERT; TRAP assay for telomerase activity; sampling 3 days post transfection.
Validation assays: TRF Southern blot on matched samples (n=14 healthy and two TB32 samples); flow-FISH on 7 RTEL1 samples.

Statistical methods: Wilcoxon rank-sum test for between-group length comparisons; linear regression for age-length summary statistics; bootstrapping for precision estimation; principal component analysis for distribution structure; R2 reporting for method-method correlations.

Machine learning: training-to-test split 7:3; features include telomere measurements and age; binary classifiers trained for (i) healthy vs symptomatic, (ii) healthy vs asymptomatic carrier, (iii) healthy vs combined cases; performance reported as accuracy and AUC.

table_plan.title: Method comparison: TRF, flow-FISH, STELA, TeSLA, TelSeq, digital nanopore
table_plan.caption_sketch: Qualitative comparison of telomere measurement methods. Values are taken verbatim from text; only numeric resolution values (30-40 bp plateau; 1000-3000 bp TRF overestimate; 1500 bp flow-FISH overestimate) are used where present.

table_plan.title: Classifier performance on healthy-vs-TBD groupings
table_plan.caption_sketch: Binary classifier performance for distinguishing healthy donors from RTEL1 symptomatic patients, asymptomatic carriers, or their union.

figure_plan.title: Method validation and precision
figure_plan.description: Classifier AUC and accuracy bar chart (from Fig. 3J); precision-vs-n bootstrapping summary (plateau at 30-40 bp).
figure_plan.caption_sketch: Bar chart comparing binary classifier accuracy (%) and AUC for three healthy-vs-TBD comparisons; precision plateau annotation.

figure_plan.title: Telomere attrition in TERT-null hESCs
figure_plan.description: Mean telomere length versus days post Cre-mediated TERT knockout at 66, 78, 98, 105 days, slope ~40 bp/day.
figure_plan.caption_sketch: Line plot of mean telomere length (bp) against days post TERT knockout (66, 78, 98, 105).

figure_plan.title: Schematic of the digital telomere measurement pipeline
figure_plan.description: Flow from genomic DNA -> telomere capture or WGS -> Oxford Nanopore -> Telometer alignment to T2T -> per-read lengths -> distribution analysis and classifier.
figure_plan.caption_sketch: Schematic of the Telometer workflow from library preparation through per-chromosome telomere length distributions.
